title: Artificial Intelligence in mental health and the biases of language based models

review_output.candidate_title: Artificial Intelligence in mental health and the biases of language based models

source_record_provenance.record_title: Artificial Intelligence in mental health and the biases of language based models

satisfied_inclusion_points: 論文為英語研究文章且提供全文（RESEARCH ARTICLE / PLOS ONE，全文可用）
論文呈現與實驗/分析相關的具體方法：對 GloVe 與 Word2Vec word embeddings 的偏誤進行分析（Euclidean distances、vector similarity/analogy 等）
論文屬於以 NLP/語言模型用於精神健康相關分析的研究
triggered_exclusion_points: 不符合 Stage 2 主題：criteria 針對「NLP 用於 process extraction（從自然語言文字抽取流程/控制流/決策模型）」；本論文重點是「精神健康領域 NLP 模型的偏誤評估（word embeddings bias）與文獻回顧」，未描述從自然語言中抽取流程模型/控制流程模型/決策模型的 process extraction 任務（如方法與結果段落僅談偏誤、嵌入距離與類比問題，而非流程模型抽取）。
屬於文獻回顧為主的設計部分：摘要與方法明確寫「A literature review...」，且主要分析是 embeddings 偏誤；不符合「具體方法與實驗以驗證 process extraction 方法」之題型要求（criteria 明確要求 process extraction 的方法與驗證）。
evidence_highlights: Abstract/Methods：A literature review of uses of NLP in mental health...；primary analysis evaluated biases within ‘GloVe’ and ‘Word2Vec’... Euclidean distances... vector similarity functions... analogy questions
Full text：3.0 Primary analysis of word embeddings；4.0 Discussion 與 recommandations 皆圍繞 word embedding/偏誤評估與建議，而非 process extraction（流程/控制流/決策模型抽取）
review_output.decision_rationale: 依 Stage 2 criteria，本次審查主題限定為「使用 NLP 進行 process extraction（從自然語言文字抽取流程/控制流或決策模型）」的系統性回顧。該論文全文與摘要聚焦於精神健康領域 NLP 模型/word embeddings 的偏誤評估，包含文獻回顧與嵌入空間距離/類比分析，未提供任何針對 process extraction（流程/控制流/決策模型抽取）之方法與實證。故判定為偏排除（stage_score=2）。

review_reasoning: 依 Stage 2 criteria，本次審查主題限定為「使用 NLP 進行 process extraction（從自然語言文字抽取流程/控制流或決策模型）」的系統性回顧。該論文全文與摘要聚焦於精神健康領域 NLP 模型/word embeddings 的偏誤評估，包含文獻回顧與嵌入空間距離/類比分析，未提供任何針對 process extraction（流程/控制流/決策模型抽取）之方法與實證。故判定為偏排除（stage_score=2）。

title: Investigating Gender Bias in BERT

triggered_exclusion_points: Paper does not specifically cover the use of NLP for process extraction from natural language text; it focuses on analyzing and mitigating gender bias in BERT for downstream emotion/sentiment intensity prediction rather than extracting process models/control-flow/decisional models.
evidence_highlights: Abstract and full text repeatedly describe ‘gender-bias induced by BERT’ and ‘downstream tasks related to emotion and sentiment intensity prediction’, using BERT embeddings with MLP regressors and ‘equity evaluation corpus’.

No section describes NLP-based process extraction (e.g., extracting process models/control-flow/decisional models) from natural language text; instead the work is about debiasing gender features in embeddings.
Although experiments are present (training MLP regressors/classifiers and evaluating with EEC), this does not match the Stage 2 topic requirement of process extraction.
review_output.decision_rationale: Stage 2 inclusion requires that the paper specifically covers NLP for process extraction from natural-language text (control-flow/decisional process models). The provided full text instead centers on gender bias in BERT and debiasing it for emotion/sentiment intensity and gender classification tasks, with no evidence of process extraction. Therefore, the candidate is excluded by the topic-mismatch exclusion criterion.

review_reasoning: Stage 2 inclusion requires that the paper specifically covers NLP for process extraction from natural-language text (control-flow/decisional process models). The provided full text instead centers on gender bias in BERT and debiasing it for emotion/sentiment intensity and gender classification tasks, with no evidence of process extraction. Therefore, the candidate is excluded by the topic-mismatch exclusion criterion.
